function_v0.doc:
Manager-controlled withdrawal.

SECURITY: the `manager` role is assumed to be a hardened multisig. This
function can move any token, any amount, and is gated only by admin
`force_pause` (not the normal `pause`). To bound the blast radius of a
partially-compromised or mistaken manager key, the recipient MUST be on
the withdraw-destination allowlist, which is itself configured through a
two-step, cooldown-gated flow (propose -> accept). Destinations can be
removed immediately. Any-token is intentional so stray/airdropped assets
can be rescued.
function_v0.name: withdraw

function_v0.doc:
Burn USDC on Stellar and initiate cross-chain transfer via CCTPv2.

Calls TokenMessengerMinterV2::deposit_for_burn which:
1. Transfers USDC from escrow to the messenger via transfer_from (requires prior approve)
2. Burns the USDC locally
3. Sends a CCTP message to the destination domain

Note: Stellar USDC uses 7 decimals. The TokenMessengerMinter handles the
decimal conversion internally (strips the 7th decimal as "dust").
function_v0.name: send_via_cctp

function_v0.doc:
Return underlying to the proxy (which forwards it to the vault and
updates its accounting atomically).

This is the INTENTIONALLY unbounded safe-return rail (L-4): it skips
the processor budget and works while paused, so an incident can be
unwound at full balance immediately, without the manager multisig or
a daily cap in the way. That is safe because the destination is
hard-wired up the trust chain \xe2\x80\x94 a compromised processor can at worst
repeatedly unwind idle liquidity (strategy denial, no extraction),
and the manager can revoke the role immediately.
function_v0.name: return_to_proxy

function_v0.doc:
Set the minimum acceptable output rate for swapping `token_in` into
`token_out`: execute_swap requires `amount_out_min >= amount_in * num
/ denom`. Both sides are in raw base units, so `num / denom` must
absorb any decimals difference between the two tokens. `num = 0`
clears the floor, which disables the pair (execute_swap fails closed
on a pair with no floor).
function_v0.name: set_swap_rate_floor

function_v0.doc: Set the cap on the processor-supplied CCTP `max_fee`, in basis points\nof the burn amount. `0` restricts sends to fee-less (standard)\ntransfers; the cap can never exceed 100%.
function_v0.name: set_cctp_max_fee_bps

function_v0.doc:
Daily execute_swap input budget for a non-underlying `token`, in that
token's base units. `0` (the default) rejects swaps spending the
token. The underlying is governed by the global processor budget
instead.
function_v0.name: set_token_daily_limit

function_v0.doc: Finalize a pending withdraw-destination addition once its cooldown elapses.
function_v0.name: accept_withdraw_destination

function_v0.doc: Remove `destination` from the withdraw allowlist immediately (no cooldown),\nso a compromised or mistaken destination can be revoked quickly.
function_v0.name: remove_withdraw_destination

function_v0.doc: Propose adding `destination` to the withdraw allowlist. Takes effect only\nafter `config_change_cooldown` via `accept_withdraw_destination`. Only one\nproposal may be pending at a time.
function_v0.name: propose_withdraw_destination

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Manager-set lower bound on the output rate of a swap pair: execute_swap
requires `amount_out_min >= amount_in * num / denom`. Both sides are in
raw base units, so `num / denom` must absorb any decimals difference
between the two tokens. A pair with no floor configured cannot be swapped.
udt_struct_v0.name: SwapRateFloor

fields.doc: Required `destination_caller` for every send. Default: zero (open\ncaller \xe2\x80\x94 anyone may execute the mint; safest, cannot strand a burn).\nImmutable after initialization (changeable only via contract upgrade).
fields.name: cctp_destination_caller

fields.doc: Cap on the CCTP `max_fee`, in bps of the burn amount.\nDefault: `DEFAULT_CCTP_MAX_FEE_BPS` (100 = 1%). Max 10_000.
fields.name: cctp_max_fee_bps

fields.doc: Highest `min_finality_threshold` send_via_cctp accepts. Default (and\nmax): `CCTP_FINALITY_FINALIZED` (2000). Set to 1000 to force the\nfast-transfer range only \xe2\x80\x94 note that bars standard transfers.
fields.name: cctp_max_finality_threshold
